_comment: Evidence-based, diet-agnostic nutrition guidance. Goal + habits lead; the original Motivated Fit Lean/Bulk meal plan is an optional template. Static reference content (no logging/tracking).
goal: A modest calorie deficit + enough protein — in whatever eating style you'll actually stick to. Aim to lose about 0.5–1% of your bodyweight per week. Slow loss keeps your muscle.

habits.title: Protein first, every meal
habits.text: Aim for ~1.6–2.2 g of protein per kg of bodyweight per day. Eat your protein and veggies before the carbs — you'll stay fuller and your energy stays steadier.

habits.title: Move after you eat
habits.text: A 2–5 minute walk after a meal noticeably lowers the blood-sugar spike. Stuck at a desk? Do seated soleus push-ups (heel raises with the ball of your foot down) — they work even sitting.

habits.title: Cut sugar & liquid calories
habits.text: Water, coffee, or tea — skip sugary drinks and added sugar. This single swap does a lot of the work.

habits.title: Mostly whole foods
habits.text: Plenty of veggies and minimally-processed food. People eat noticeably less without trying when they cut ultra-processed food.

habits.title: Pick a pattern you'll keep
habits.text: Keto, intermittent fasting, or set meals all work IF the above are met. The best diet is the one you actually stick to — consistency beats 'perfect'.

habits.title: Sleep & steps
habits.text: 7–9 hours of sleep and daily steps quietly drive fat loss and crush cravings. Don't underrate them.

dinnerCarbNote: Optional lever: going lighter on carbs at dinner helps many people control total intake. It's a handy tactic — not a rule. Calories matter far more than carb timing.

lean.name: Lean — meal template
lean.summary: 4 meals + snacks · lower carb & fat, higher protein · dinner has no carbs.

meals.name: Breakfast
meals.items: 1 protein + 1 carb + 1 fruit (e.g. 2 eggs · ½ cup oats or 1 slice wheat toast · 1 apple)

meals.name: Snacks (all, daily)
meals.items: 2 hard-boiled eggs · ½ avocado · ⅓ cup almonds (or Greek yogurt) · 1 apple/fruit

lean.carbs: Brown rice ½ cup · Sweet potato 4oz · Quinoa ½ cup · Wheat bread 1½ slices · Wheat pasta ⅔ cup (~22–25g each)

bulk.name: Bulk — meal template
bulk.summary: 5 meals + snacks · moderate-to-high across all macros · include 1 lean-beef or salmon meal daily.

meals.name: Breakfast
meals.items: 1 protein + 1 carb + 1 fruit (e.g. 3 eggs · ½ cup oats · 1 apple)

meals.name: Snacks (all, daily)
meals.items: 2 hard-boiled eggs · ½ avocado · ⅓ cup almonds · 1 apple/fruit

bulk.carbs: Brown rice ⅔ cup · Sweet potato 5oz · Quinoa ⅔ cup · Wheat bread 2 slices · Wheat pasta 1 cup (~30g each)

proteins: Chicken breast 3.5oz · Ground turkey 5.5oz · Lean beef 4oz · Salmon 5.5oz · White fish 4oz (~30g protein each)
veggies: 1 cup of any (5 cups leafy greens = 1 cup): asparagus, broccoli, bell peppers, Brussels sprouts, spinach, kale, cauliflower, zucchini, mixed greens

grocery.items: Eggs · Chicken breast · Ground turkey · Lean beef · Salmon · White fish
Brown rice · Sweet potato · Oats · Quinoa · Wheat bread · Wheat pasta
Asparagus · Broccoli · Bell peppers · Brussels sprouts · Spinach · Kale · Cauliflower · Zucchini · Mixed greens
Apples · Bananas · Berries · Cantaloupe · Pineapple · Grapefruit
Avocado · Almonds · Avocado oil · Olive oil · Coconut oil · Greek yogurt · Chia seeds

rules: Cook only with avocado, olive, or coconut oil.
No added sugar. Go easy on salt — use pepper, cumin, curry, herbs.
Drink only water (coffee/tea black is fine).
If the plan says "or", pick one option.
Eat all your snacks daily — spread them out.
It doesn't matter how late your last meal is, as long as it fits the plan.
